title: Ixchelia uxpanapana
taxonomy: Ixchelia uxpanapana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family comprises around 5,000 species of plants, many of which are tropical or subtropical in distribution. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Central America. It is a climbing vine or shrub that produces small, white flowers. Ixchelia uxpanapana is a relatively rare species, and its classification within the Apocynaceae family is well established.

Ixchelia uxpanapana has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
